stories.name: Course catalog
stories.description: Authenticated users browse a catalog of published courses, each with name, image and details, and open a course to see its episodes.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Course catalog
  Scenario: Active user browses courses
    Given an active user "Dana"
    And a published course "React Basics" with details "Learn React"
    When Dana visits the courses page
    Then Dana sees the course "React Basics"
    And Dana can open the course "React Basics"

stories.name: Episodes: movie and quiz kinds
stories.description: A course has many ordered episodes. Each episode is either a movie (with transcript) or a quiz.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Episodes
  Scenario: Course lists its episodes in order
    Given a published course "React Basics"
    And the course has a movie episode "Intro" at position 1
    And the course has a quiz episode "Checkpoint" at position 2
    When an active user opens the course "React Basics"
    Then the episodes appear in order "Intro, Checkpoint"

stories.name: Invitation and activation
stories.description: Admin invites a user by company email; the user receives an email, clicks the activation link, sets a password and becomes active.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Invitation and activation
  Scenario: Invited user activates account
    Given an admin invites "newuser@comtecglobal.com"
    Then an invitation email is sent to "newuser@comtecglobal.com"
    When the user opens the activation link and sets password "secret123"
    Then the user "newuser@comtecglobal.com" is active
    And the user can sign in with password "secret123"

stories.name: Roles: student vs admin
stories.description: Users have a role. Only admins may reach the /admin area; students are redirected.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Roles
  Scenario: Student cannot access admin
    Given an active student "Dana"
    When Dana visits the admin dashboard
    Then Dana is denied access
  Scenario: Admin can access admin
    Given an active admin "Assaf"
    When Assaf visits the admin dashboard
    Then Assaf sees the admin dashboard

stories.name: Quiz answer capture
stories.description: For a quiz episode the UI presents the question and stores the user's answer for later use.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Quiz answers
  Scenario: User answers a quiz
    Given an active user "Dana" on a quiz episode asking "2+2?"
    When Dana submits the answer "4"
    Then Dana's answer "4" is stored for that quiz

stories.name: Movie playback
stories.description: For a movie episode the UI shows the video player and the transcript.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Movie playback
  Scenario: User watches a movie episode
    Given an active user "Dana" on a movie episode "Intro" with a movie attached
    When Dana opens the episode
    Then Dana sees a video player
    And Dana sees the transcript

stories.name: Per-episode chat
stories.description: Every episode has a chat-style Q&A thread; each message is stored per user and episode.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Episode chat
  Scenario: User posts a question in the episode chat
    Given an active user "Dana" on an episode "Intro"
    When Dana posts the chat message "What is a component?"
    Then the chat thread for Dana on "Intro" contains "What is a component?"

stories.name: Markdown documents on episodes
stories.description: An episode may have one or more named markdown documents, each with a name and content, shown rendered.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Markdown docs
  Scenario: Episode shows attached markdown
    Given an episode "Intro" has a markdown doc named "Notes" with content "# Hello"
    When an active user opens the episode "Intro"
    Then the user sees a document "Notes"
    And the rendered document contains a heading "Hello"

stories.name: RTL Hebrew UI
stories.description: The UI is in Hebrew and all screens are right-to-left.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: RTL Hebrew UI
  Scenario: Pages render right-to-left in Hebrew
    Given an active user "Dana"
    When Dana visits the courses page
    Then the page html has dir "rtl"
    And the page html has lang "he"

stories.name: Mobile Bulma / Web Bootstrap split
stories.description: Mobile visitors get the Bulma layout; web visitors get the Bootstrap layout; a query override forces either.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Responsive framework split
  Scenario: Mobile visitor gets Bulma
    Given an active user "Dana"
    When Dana visits the courses page with view "mobile"
    Then the page uses the Bulma stylesheet
  Scenario: Web visitor gets Bootstrap
    Given an active user "Dana"
    When Dana visits the courses page with view "web"
    Then the page uses the Bootstrap stylesheet

stories.name: Admin: user management
stories.description: Admins list users, invite new ones, change roles and deactivate accounts.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Admin user management
  Scenario: Admin invites a new user
    Given an active admin "Assaf"
    When Assaf invites the user "newuser@comtecglobal.com"
    Then "newuser@comtecglobal.com" appears in the users list with status "invited"

stories.name: Admin: activity tracking
stories.description: User activities (episode views, quiz answers, logins) are recorded and shown to admins.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Activity tracking
  Scenario: Admin sees a user's activity
    Given an active user "Dana" who viewed the episode "Intro"
    And an active admin "Assaf"
    When Assaf opens the activity dashboard
    Then Assaf sees an activity "viewed episode" for "Dana"

stories.name: Admin: episode content editing
stories.description: Admins create and edit episodes and fill their content: name, kind, title, movie_url, audiobook_url, transcript, plus quiz questions and markdown docs.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Episode content editing
  Scenario: Admin edits episode content
    Given an active admin "Assaf" and a course "React Basics"
    When Assaf adds a movie episode titled "Intro" with transcript "hello world"
    Then the course "React Basics" has an episode "Intro" with transcript "hello world"

stories.name: AI content generation (stub)
stories.description: Admins trigger AI generation that fills an episode's name, kind, title, movie_url, audiobook_url and transcript. Backed by a swappable service that starts as a stub.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: AI content generation
  Scenario: Admin generates episode content with AI
    Given an active admin "Assaf" and an empty episode in "React Basics"
    When Assaf generates content with AI for the episode
    Then the episode has a title
    And the episode has a transcript

stories.name: Hebrew TTS podcast (stub)
stories.description: Admins generate a Hebrew audio podcast from a transcript via a swappable TTS provider (ElevenLabs), starting as a stub that attaches placeholder audio.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Hebrew TTS podcast
  Scenario: Admin generates Hebrew audio
    Given an active admin "Assaf" and an episode with a transcript
    When Assaf generates the Hebrew podcast for the episode
    Then the episode has an audio attachment

stories.name: ffmpeg studio and movie assembly
stories.description: Admin studio assembles an episode movie with ffmpeg from a thumbnail/slide plus audio plus burned captions, mixes background music with volume control, generates a thumbnail and prepends an animated intro title card.
stories.cucumber_test:
Feature: Studio movie assembly
  Scenario: Admin assembles a movie
    Given an active admin "Assaf" and an episode with audio and a thumbnail
    When Assaf assembles the movie in the studio
    Then the episode has a movie attachment

stories.name: Projects & documents (admin)
stories.description: Admins organise AI-generated markdown documents under projects; each project carries a fixed set of doc types, editable in admin.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/admin_projects.feature

stories.name: Documents API (shared secret)
stories.description: A shared-secret JSON API lets an agent create projects and upsert documents by type; wrong/missing key rejected.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/documents_api.feature

stories.name: Brandable document rendering
stories.description: Rendered markdown documents carry the company brand colours, fonts and logo via an admin-editable theme.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/branded_docs.feature

stories.name: The Library page
stories.description: Cross-project document hub with Recent, Popular (most viewed) and My favorites sections; readable by all signed-in users.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/library.feature

stories.name: Office upload to markdown
stories.description: Admins upload Word/Excel; the file is converted to markdown (LibreOffice + reverse_markdown) and the original is kept as an attachment.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/office_upload.feature

stories.name: Favorite documents
stories.description: Users favorite/unfavorite documents; favorites show in the Library.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/favorites.feature

stories.name: Library search & tags
stories.description: Search/filter documents in the Library by name, project, kind (doc_type) and freeform tags; tags editable via admin form, upload and API.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/library_search.feature

stories.name: Project members & avatars
stories.description: Users belong to projects (many-to-many); non-admin users imported from an Excel roster with avatars; project pages show an overlapping avatar stack.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/project_members.feature

stories.name: Presentation/movie builder
stories.description: DAW-style builder: markdown screenplay -> slides (text/code, Hebrew+English) with per-slide TTS voice-over, background music and captions; export PDF (Chrome) and narrated MP4 (ffmpeg); 4-track timeline + preview.
stories.cucumber_test: see features/presentations.feature
